title: cinchona lancifolia var. vera
intro: Cinchona lancifolia var. vera, also known as the Andean cinchona, is a type of evergreen tree native to the Andean region of South America. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to combat fever, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimalarial agent. The health benefits of Cinchona lancifolia var. vera include its potential to alleviate symptoms of malaria, as well as its ability to provide relief from headaches and other fever-related ailments. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through the extraction of its bark, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions.
